hallucinations.description: The paper cites numerous non-existent academic papers with fabricated 2025 dates and arXiv IDs (e.g., Mündler et al. 2025 arXiv:2504.09246, Li et al. 2025a arXiv:2510.17376, Zeng et al. 2025 arXiv:2510.06296, and others). These references cannot be verified and do not correspond to real publications.
hallucinations.evidence: References section: 'Mündler, N., ... (2025). Type-Constrained Code Generation with Language Models. arXiv:2504.09246.' and similar entries for Li et al. (2025a,b,c), Aggarwal et al. (2025), etc.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found, primarily in the form of nonexistent citations to fabricated 2025 papers. The experimental results and methodology appear consistent with the provided source code execution logs, with no evidence of faked results or mathematical errors.
